title: Death of Misatos Alpine Renault
wikitext:
[]: Death of Misato's Alpine Renault             Quote Post by vincentb » Mon Jul 13, 2020 3:11 am I'm trying to remember in which episode/movie Misato's car (Alpine Renault) was in an accident and crashed. In Evangelion 1:11 it does have an accident, but it is not really damaged, but I seem to remember that at one point it is partially destroyed. Can anyone help me find that moment again? Thanx

[]: Re: Death of Misato's Alpine Renault             Quote Post by Derantor » Mon Jul 13, 2020 6:39 am It gets shot up and crashes in EoE, while she's trying to get Shinji into the robot. Also, Episode 1. Gets turned over by the N2-Mine explosion.

[]: Re: Death of Misato's Alpine Renault             Quote Post by vincentb » Mon Jul 13, 2020 7:15 am Thank you very much! That was exactly the EoE sequence I had in mind (at 28 minutes from the start).

[]: Re: Death of Misato's Alpine Renault             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Jul 13, 2020 7:27 pm Well, it also gets completely wrecked in 2.22, and she replaces it with some other car. (I don't know the model, but it's a very sporty-looking car with GunBuster pilot suit colors painted on it.)

[]: Re: Death of Misato's Alpine Renault             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jul 13, 2020 7:42 pm Derantor wrote: get Shinji into the robot.

[]: Re: Death of Misato's Alpine Renault             Quote Post by Gus Hanson » Mon Jul 13, 2020 7:58 pm You could say that with the loss of the car, another piece of Misato's soul was lost that fateful day. I don't remember if somebody said something of that effect in the EoE commentary...

[]: Re: Death of Misato's Alpine Renault             Quote Post by vincentb » Tue Jul 14, 2020 2:53 am Misato's "new" car in 2.22 is a 1967 Mazda Cosmo 110. So she goes from a car of the 1980's to a car of the 1960's Linking Misato's death with the death of her car in EoE is indeed an interesting motive: just as the child pilots are viscerally linked to their Eva machine, Misato is also linked to a machine with which she is one. Misato is a pilot herself! Obviously piloting a sports car is not the same as piloting an eva, which underlines the difference in nature between the adult world (linked to "small" mechanical machines) and the children's world (linked to "big" living machines) in evangelion.

[]: Re: Death of Misato's Alpine Renault             Quote Post by Derantor » Tue Jul 14, 2020 6:34 am Reichu wrote: BEGONE WITH THAT ACCURSED MEME! *burns the thread and throws salt everywhere*

[]: Re: Death of Misato's Alpine Renault             Quote Post by C.T.1290 » Mon Jul 20, 2020 1:53 pm The saddest part is that she never got it fully paid off. She had some payments left, and now it’s totaled. We have to pay our respects for that car.
